{"rule":"OXFORD_SPELLING_Z_NOT_S","sentence":"^\\QCUDA-accelerated batch k-mer insert and query from sequences\nConfigurable k-mer length, minimiser width, s-mer width, and hash function count\nMinimizer-based shard selection for cache-efficient streaming queries\nFindere false-positive reduction via overlapping s-mer membership\nHeader-only library design\nFASTA/FASTQ stream and file support\\E$"}
{"rule":"OXFORD_SPELLING_Z_NOT_S","sentence":"^\\QIt exploits the streaming nature of sequence-derived k-mers by using minimisers to group consecutive k-mers sharing the same minimiser into super-k-mers, assigning all k-mers of a super-k-mer to the same 256-bit memory shard.\\E$"}
{"rule":"OXFORD_SPELLING_Z_NOT_S","sentence":"^\\QThis library provides a GPU-accelerated Bloom filter optimised for high-throughput batch k-mer insertion and query on nucleotide (DNA) and protein sequences (or any other sequence type as long as a valid alphabet is provided).\\E$"}
{"rule":"OXFORD_SPELLING_Z_NOT_S","sentence":"^\\QThis library provides a high-performance GPU adaption of the Super Bloom filter optimised for high-throughput batch k-mer insertion and query on nucleotide (DNA) and protein sequences (or any other sequence type as long as a valid alphabet is provided).\\E$"}
